title: Canonical Backlog Model Independent of External Platforms
description: The system shall maintain a canonical backlog representation of AI-generated requirements that is independent of any external project management platform. External platforms (Jira, Azure DevOps) are downstream execution systems; the AI Requirements platform owns the canonical requirement and traceability model.

architectureDecision.canonicalHierarchy: Epic → Feature → User Story → Acceptance Criteria

title: Epic → Feature → User Story → Acceptance Criteria Hierarchy
description: The system shall represent AI-generated requirements using an Epic → Feature → User Story → Acceptance Criteria hierarchy. All four levels shall be maintained in the canonical backlog model.

stories.title: Full-Time Employee Eligibility
acceptanceCriteria: Coverage begins first day of following month

title: JSON Export of Complete Canonical Backlog
description: The system shall support JSON export of the complete canonical backlog. The JSON export shall preserve the full information model including hierarchy, acceptance criteria, requirement IDs, source references, and traceability.

title: CSV Export for Business Review
description: The system shall support CSV export that flattens the hierarchy for business review and bulk processing. CSV columns: Epic, Feature, Story ID, User Story, Priority, Status, Acceptance Criteria, Requirement IDs, Source.

title: Configurable Export Adapters for External Platforms
description: The system shall support configurable export adapters for external project management platforms. Adapter configuration shall include field mapping, hierarchy mapping, and custom field definitions.

title: Jira-Compatible Backlog Export
description: The system shall support Jira-compatible backlog export using a dedicated adapter. Mapping: Canonical Epic → Jira Epic; Canonical Feature → Jira Story or custom issue type; User Story → Jira Story; Acceptance Criteria → Description/AC field; Requirement ID, Source Document, AI Confidence → Jira custom fields.

title: Azure DevOps Backlog Export as Configurable Integration
description: The system shall support Azure DevOps-compatible backlog export as a configurable integration. Mapping: Canonical Epic → ADO Epic; Feature → ADO Feature; User Story → ADO User Story; Acceptance Criteria → ADO Acceptance Criteria; Requirement ID → ADO tag or custom field.

title: Preserve Traceability During Export
description: The system shall preserve requirement IDs, source document references, acceptance criteria, and traceability information during all export formats. Traceability shall not be lost in any export adapter.

title: Maintain External System Identifiers for Exported Items
description: The system shall maintain external system identifiers for each exported backlog item. For Jira exports, the Jira issue key shall be stored. For Azure DevOps, the work item ID shall be stored. External IDs shall be linked to the canonical backlog item.

title: Prevent Duplicate Issue Creation on Repeated Exports
description: The system shall prevent duplicate external issue creation during repeated exports by checking existing external IDs before creating new items. Items with an existing external ID shall be updated, not duplicated.

title: Full and Incremental Export Support
description: The system shall support full export (all items) and incremental exports. Incremental options: Export New (items created since last export), Export Changed (items modified since last export), Export Approved (items with APPROVED status only).

title: Configurable Export Scope
description: The system shall allow users to select export scope: all items, approved items only, newly created items, or changed items. The export UI shall display item counts before export confirmation.

title: Backlog Completeness Validation Before Export
description: The system shall validate backlog completeness before export, including: all epics present, all stories have acceptance criteria, traceability coverage percentage, and count of items requiring human review. Unresolved high-severity conflicts shall generate a warning before export is allowed.

title: Export Success, Failure, and Retry Status
description: The system shall report export results: total items attempted, created, updated, failed. Failed items shall include a reason code. Users shall be able to retry failed items or download an error report.

title: Audit Trail for All Backlog Exports and External Integrations
description: The system shall maintain an audit trail for all backlog export events, including: export ID, format, items exported, exported-by actor, export timestamp, target system, and status.

title: Retain Export Snapshot and Version
description: The system shall retain the requirement snapshot and version used to generate each export. The snapshot shall be accessible after the export to support post-export audit and comparison.
